_comment: Tier-1 skill-contract manifest. Each prose invariant is a regex asserted (case-insensitive, DOTALL) against the named file by tests/test_skill_contract.py. These guardrails live ONLY in SKILL.md prose with no other automated guard; deleting one would otherwise pass CI green. Adding a new guardrail to the skill = add an entry here. The `rationale` is surfaced on failure so a breakage is self-explaining.

prose.rationale: linkedin_post.py has NO approval check in code; approval-before-publish lives only in this prose. Deleting it lets the agent publish to the user's real profile without consent.

SKILL.md must reference the source gate at all (the literal appears in the Generate research step, the Publish gate note, and Guardrails). This is a soft check that the gate isn't unreferenced; the HARD enforcement that publishing runs the gate is code-tested in tests/test_linkedin_post.py (enforce_source_gate) — not this regex.
LinkedIn ToS §3.1 exposure: this is the only instruction telling the agent to decline scheduled/looped/unattended posting.

prose.pattern: (never put sources[\s\S]{0,60}?post\s+body|sources stay in the sidecar)
prose.rationale: verify_sources never inspects the post body; keeping sources out of the body is prose-only (and in-body links also crush reach).
--allow-unverified is the single bypass of the source gate; it is enforced only by this human-only convention.
Anti-fabrication for first-person content has no mechanical check; this is the skill's core authenticity rule.
Generate flow ordering is load-bearing: research must run AFTER Save (the slug/sidecar path exists) and BEFORE Show (the shown draft is already source-backed).
